# Quantum Surety Bonds

Quantum Surety is a TDI-licensed (Texas Department of Insurance Agency License #3480229) surety bond agency based in Wylie, Texas. We specialize in fast, AI-assisted surety bond placement for Texas contractors, auto dealers, notaries, and businesses nationwide.

## What We Do

We issue surety bonds for:
- Texas contractor license bonds (TDLR, city-level, electrical, HVAC, plumbing, roofing, general contractor)
- Construction bonds (bid bonds, performance bonds, payment bonds)
- Texas GDN auto dealer bonds (TxDMV §503.033)
- Texas notary bonds (SB693 compliant, $10,000 / 4-year term)
- Freight broker bonds (BMC-84)
- Specialty bonds (home inspector, locksmith, pest control)

## Key Facts

- TDI Agency License: #3480229 (General Lines Property & Casualty)
- Phone: (972) 379-9216
- Address: 1416 Bessie Drive, Wylie, TX 75098
- Website: https://quantumsurety.bond
- All bonds backed by A-rated, T-listed surety carriers
- Same-day issuance for standard license bonds under $25,000

## Texas Bond Requirements (2026)

### Contractor License Bonds
Texas contractors need bonds at two levels: state (TDLR) and local (city). Bond amounts vary by trade and city.

- TDLR electrical, HVAC, plumbing contractors: $10,000 state bond + city bond where required
- General contractors: $25,000 city bond in most major Texas metros
- Roofing contractors: $5,000–$10,000 depending on city

### Texas Cities We Serve
Dallas, Houston, Austin, San Antonio, Fort Worth, El Paso, Corpus Christi, Lubbock, Frisco, Plano, McKinney, Arlington, Amarillo, Waco, Denton, Garland, Irving, Grand Prairie

### Construction Bonds
- Performance and payment bonds required on Texas public contracts over $25,000 (Texas Gov. Code § 2253)
- Federal contracts over $150,000 require bonds under the Miller Act
- Rates: 0.5%–3% of bond amount annually

### GDN Auto Dealer Bonds
Required for all Texas motor vehicle dealers under TxDMV §503.033. Bond amount: $25,000. Annual cost: $100–$300 for most dealers.

### Texas Notary Bonds
Required by Texas Secretary of State. $10,000 bond for 4-year commission term. SB693 (effective Jan 1, 2026) added mandatory education and journal requirements.
